title: Cell Theory
content: Cell Theory states that all living organisms are composed of cells, the cell is the basic unit of life, and all cells arise from pre-existing cells.

title: Prokaryotic Cell
content: Prokaryotic cells are simple cells without a nucleus or membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are examples of prokaryotes.

title: Eukaryotic Cell
content: Eukaryotic cells contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Plants, animals, fungi, and protists are eukaryotic organisms.

title: Mitosis
content: Mitosis is cell division that produces two genetically identical daughter cells. It is responsible for growth and tissue repair.

title: Meiosis
content: Meiosis is a type of cell division that produces four genetically different haploid cells. It is essential for sexual reproduction.

title: DNA Structure
content: DNA has a double helix structure composed of two strands held together by complementary base pairing between adenine-thymine and cytosine-guanine.

title: RNA
content: RNA is a nucleic acid involved in protein synthesis. It differs from DNA by containing ribose sugar and uracil instead of thymine.

title: Protein Synthesis
content: Protein synthesis occurs in two stages: transcription, where DNA is converted to mRNA, and translation, where ribosomes use mRNA to assemble amino acids into proteins.

title: Enzyme
content: Enzymes are biological catalysts that speed up chemical reactions in cells by lowering activation energy.

title: Photosynthesis
content: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy. The simplified equation is 6CO2 + 6H2O → C6H12O6 + 6O2.

title: Cellular Respiration
content: Cellular respiration converts glucose into energy in the form of ATP. The overall equation is C6H12O6 + 6O2 → 6CO2 + 6H2O + ATP.

title: ATP
content: ATP (adenosine triphosphate) is the primary energy currency of cells. It stores and transfers energy within biological systems.

title: Natural Selection
content: Natural selection is the process by which individuals with advantageous traits are more likely to survive and reproduce.

title: Evolution
content: Evolution is the gradual change in the genetic composition of populations over generations through mechanisms such as mutation and natural selection.

title: Ecosystem
content: An ecosystem consists of living organisms interacting with each other and their physical environment.

title: Food Chain
content: A food chain shows the flow of energy from producers to consumers to decomposers.

title: Homeostasis
content: Homeostasis is the ability of an organism to maintain stable internal conditions despite external changes.

title: Diffusion
content: Diffusion is the passive movement of molecules from a region of higher concentration to lower concentration.

title: Osmosis
content: Osmosis is the diffusion of water across a semipermeable membrane from low solute concentration to high solute concentration.

title: Active Transport
content: Active transport moves substances against their concentration gradient using energy, typically ATP.

title: Genetics
content: Genetics is the study of heredity and variation in organisms.

title: Dominant and Recessive Traits
content: Dominant traits are expressed when at least one dominant allele is present, while recessive traits require two recessive alleles.

title: Punnett Square
content: A Punnett square is a diagram used to predict genetic outcomes of crosses between individuals.

title: Mutation
content: A mutation is a change in DNA sequence. Mutations can be beneficial, harmful, or neutral.

title: Biodiversity
content: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life forms within an ecosystem or on Earth as a whole.

title: Taxonomy
content: Taxonomy is the science of classifying organisms into hierarchical groups such as domain, kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.

title: Binomial Nomenclature
content: Binomial nomenclature is the two-part scientific naming system for organisms, consisting of genus and species names.

title: Nervous System
content: The nervous system controls and coordinates body activities using electrical and chemical signals. It consists of the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.

title: Endocrine System
content: The endocrine system regulates body functions through hormones released into the bloodstream by glands.

title: Immune System
content: The immune system protects the body against pathogens such as bacteria and viruses using specialized cells and antibodies.

title: Antibody
content: Antibodies are proteins produced by B cells that recognize and bind to specific antigens to neutralize pathogens.

title: Antigen
content: An antigen is a substance that triggers an immune response, often found on the surface of pathogens.

title: Circulatory System
content: The circulatory system transports oxygen, nutrients, hormones, and waste products through blood pumped by the heart.

title: Respiratory System
content: The respiratory system enables gas exchange, supplying oxygen to the blood and removing carbon dioxide.

title: Digestive System
content: The digestive system breaks down food into nutrients that can be absorbed and used by the body.

title: Excretion
content: Excretion is the removal of metabolic waste products from the body to maintain homeostasis.

title: Hormone
content: Hormones are chemical messengers released by endocrine glands that regulate physiological processes.

title: Reproduction
content: Reproduction is the biological process by which organisms produce offspring, either sexually or asexually.

title: Asexual Reproduction
content: Asexual reproduction produces offspring genetically identical to the parent without gamete fusion.

title: Sexual Reproduction
content: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of gametes from two parents, producing genetically diverse offspring.

title: Chromosome
content: Chromosomes are structures composed of DNA and proteins that carry genetic information.

title: Gene
content: A gene is a segment of DNA that codes for a specific protein or functional RNA molecule.

title: Allele
content: An allele is a variant form of a gene found at a specific locus on a chromosome.

title: Transcription
content: Transcription is the process by which DNA is copied into messenger RNA (mRNA).

title: Translation
content: Translation is the process by which ribosomes read mRNA to synthesize proteins.

title: Climate Change (Biological Impact)
content: Climate change affects biodiversity, ecosystems, and species distribution due to shifts in temperature and weather patterns.

title: Population Growth
content: Population growth describes changes in population size over time, influenced by birth rates, death rates, and migration.

title: Carrying Capacity
content: Carrying capacity is the maximum population size an environment can sustainably support.

title: Symbiosis
content: Symbiosis is a close relationship between two species. Types include mutualism, commensalism, and parasitism.

title: Adaptation
content: An adaptation is a heritable trait that increases an organism’s chances of survival and reproduction in a specific environment.
